summary: Rewrite reminders create/cancel to call /v3/userdata.write with the full reminder value shape recovered from a live snoozed thread.
reason: The generator-emitted /reminders/create endpoint returns 400 "missing threadId" on every body variant against ingested threads. The Superhuman web client snoozes threads via /v3/userdata.write writing to users/<userId>/threads/<threadId>/reminder with value {clientCreatedAt, keepOnReply, messageIds, onDesktop, reminderId, source: "USER", threadId, triggerAt} (all required; recovered by reading a real reminder back via userdata.read). cancel writes null to the same path -- Superhuman's standard delete-via-null-write convention, mirrors drafts_discard. The CLI now does userdata.read first to discover the thread's messageIds, generates a UUID for reminderId, formats triggerAt as ISO-with-nanos UTC, and maps --condition {always,if-no-reply} to the keepOnReply bool. --reminder-id is retained as a deprecated alias of --thread-id for back-compat. Full discovery + before/after wire shapes in .manuscripts/20260515-165115/discovery/reminders-sniff-report.md.
validated_outcome: Live API: reminders create + reminders cancel both return HTTP 200 with currentHistoryId advance against mvh@esperlabs.ai. Unit tests: TestRemindersCreate_BackendCallShape pins the full 8-field value shape + sorted messageIds + UUID reminderId; full ./internal/cli suite passes (289 tests).
